title: Fluctuation-driven chiral ferromagnetism
authors: Rokas Veitas, Ahmed Khalifa, Francisco Machado, Shubhayu Chatterjee
abstract: In general, quantum fluctuations are suppressed in ferromagnetic materials because they admit a simple unfrustrated ground state, greatly limiting the scope of phenomena that can be observed in these materials. In this work, we show how magnetization-non-conserving couplings fundamentally alter this paradigm by demonstrating the existence of a chiral ferromagnet that is stabilized by quantum fluctuations. More specifically, we show how these spin-orbit interactions modify the classical phase diagram; whereas a classical analysis predicts only collinear states, we observe fluctuation-stabilized phases, including a ferromagnet with large orbital chirality and a chiral stripe regime. We elucidate how such couplings spontaneously generate a scalar orbital chirality, in contrast to conventional mechanisms which rely upon a field-induced canting of vector chiral order. The resultant chiral states exhibit distinct transport signatures, namely an enhanced thermal Hall effect, and are of direct relevance to moiré heterostructures, Rydberg-atom arrays, and solid-state materials featuring non-Kramers spins.
summary: This paper shows that spin-orbit interactions can induce a chiral ferromagnetic phase stabilized by quantum fluctuations, even when classical models predict only collinear states. This mechanism generates spontaneous scalar orbital chirality and leads to an enhanced thermal Hall effect. The findings are applicable to a variety of platforms including moiré materials and Rydberg-atom arrays.
main_problem: Investigating how magnetization-non-conserving couplings (spin-orbit interactions) can overcome the suppression of quantum fluctuations in ferromagnetic materials to stabilize new phases.
main_result: The discovery of a chiral ferromagnet and a chiral stripe regime that are stabilized by quantum fluctuations, characterized by spontaneous scalar orbital chirality.
method: Theoretical analysis of the modification of the classical phase diagram by spin-orbit interactions and the evaluation of fluctuation-induced effects.
model_or_system: A ferromagnetic system featuring magnetization-non-conserving couplings and spin-orbit interactions, relevant to moire heterostructures, Rydberg-atom arrays, and non-Kramers spin materials.
key_observables: Orbital chirality, scalar orbital chirality, and the thermal Hall effect.
important_parameters: Magnetization-non-conserving couplings, spin-orbit interactions, and the presence of quantum fluctuations.
main_assumptions: The study assumes the presence of spin-orbit interactions that do not conserve magnetization.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of suppressed fluctuations in ferromagnets, presents the mechanism of fluctuation-stabilized chiral phases via spin-orbit coupling, describes the resulting phase diagram (including chiral stripes), and discusses transport signatures and experimental relevance.
why_interesting: The paper demonstrates how quantum fluctuations can actively stabilize novel topological/chiral phases rather than just destroying order, which is highly relevant to many-body dynamics and Rydberg-atom arrays.
